intro: Volatile AI stocks saw broad selling pressure, prompting investors to reassess valuations across the sector. The US AI Safety Institute's head resigned after three months in the role, marking another leadership shift in the Trump administration's AI governance efforts. Alibaba unveiled Qwen3.8, claiming it ranks second globally behind Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 in capability.

whitelist.title: Here's our advice for investors looking to buy the dip in volatile AI stocks

whitelist.title: US AI safety agency head resigns three months after being appointed.
whitelist.desc: Chris Fall was picked to be the Trump administration’s leader of the US Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI, previously called the AI Safety Institute until it was renamed last year) in April, but now CNBC and Reuters report he is out. The reasons for his departure are unclear, as is the role of CAISI in the administration’s constantly shifting priorities around AI.
